title: Boelia sphaerocarpa
taxonomy: Boelia sphaerocarpa is classified within the plant family Asclepiadaceae. This family is a part of the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Boelia sphaerocarpa also includes its kingdom, which is Plantae. Further, it is often grouped into the subclass Asteridae within the class Magnoliopsida. intro: Boelia sphaerocarpa, also known as Bois de Chine or Bois de Chine Tree, is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae. The plant is traditionally used in medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Boelia sphaerocarpa include alkaloids, terpenoids, and other compounds, such as annonaceous acetogenins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Boelia sphaerocarpa is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal benefits.
